Details: In a bench-scale study assessing PFOA, removal with UV (220-460nm) in combination with H2O2 was not significant. The study reported 35 percent removal after 24 hours of irradiation; removal was less effective in comparison to UV alone (45 percent) [1701]. In pilot [2659, 2847] and full-scale [2441] studies, advanced oxidation with ultraviolet irradiation and hydrogen peroxide was ineffective for removing any of the PFASs sampled.
